title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
author: Stuart Russell & Peter Norvig
description: Comprehensive introduction to the theory and practice of artificial intelligence.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: Hawking explores the secrets of the universe, from black holes to general relativity.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
description: Peter Thiel shows how we can find singular ways to create new things.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Bilbo Baggins embarks on an unexpected adventure with a wizard and dwarves.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: How did our species succeed in the battle for dominance? A sweeping history of humankind.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship for writing clean, maintainable code.

title: The Innovators
author: Walter Isaacson
description: The story of the people who created the computer and the Internet.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A groundbreaking tour of the mind explaining the two systems that drive the way we think.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: The story of the fabulously wealthy Jay Gatsby and his love for Daisy Buchanan.

title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
description: Ancient Chinese military treatise on strategy and tactics.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: David Thomas & Andrew Hunt
description: Examines the core process of software development.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian cautionary tale about totalitarianism.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A tale of racial injustice in Depression-era Alabama.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A magical story about following your dreams.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: How entrepreneurs use continuous innovation to create successful businesses.

title: Good to Great
author: Jim Collins
description: Why some companies make the leap and others don't.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
description: Timeless lessons on wealth, greed, and happiness.

title: Steve Jobs
author: Walter Isaacson
description: The exclusive biography of Steve Jobs based on forty interviews.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: Personal writings of the Roman Emperor on Stoic philosophy.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A personal voyage through the universe exploring astronomy and physics.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A groundbreaking exposition of evolutionary theory from a gene-centric perspective.

title: The Origin of Species
author: Charles Darwin
description: Darwin's landmark work on evolutionary biology and natural selection.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: A sweeping tale of politics, religion and ecology in a far-future empire.

title: Ender's Game
author: Orson Scott Card
description: A gifted child is trained to be Earth's greatest military commander.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
description: A comedic science fiction series following Arthur Dent through the universe.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A futuristic dystopia where citizens are engineered for social stability.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: A fireman whose job is to burn books questions his duty.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A psychological portrait of a young man who commits a murder and struggles with guilt.

title: The Code Book
author: Simon Singh
description: The evolution of secrecy from Mary Queen of Scots to quantum cryptography.

title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
author: Dale Carnegie
description: The landmark bestseller on communication and interpersonal skills.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
author: Robert Kiyosaki
description: What the rich teach their kids about money that the poor and middle class do not.

title: Think and Grow Rich
author: Napoleon Hill
description: The philosophy of success distilled from interviews with over 500 millionaires.

title: The Power of Now
author: Eckhart Tolle
description: A guide to spiritual enlightenment — living fully in the present moment.

title: The Republic
author: Plato
description: Plato's seminal work on justice, the just city-state and the just man.

title: Design Patterns
author: Gang of Four
description: Elements of reusable object-oriented software — the classic patterns reference.

title: Introduction to Algorithms
author: Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest & Stein
description: The comprehensive textbook covering a broad range of algorithms in depth.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: Holden Caulfield's experiences in New York City after being expelled from school.

title: Homo Deus
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A brief history of tomorrow — what happens when humanity transcends biological limits.

title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
author: Stephen Covey
description: Powerful lessons in personal change through principles of fairness and integrity.

title: The Phoenix Project
author: Gene Kim, Kevin Behr & George Spafford
description: A novel about IT, DevOps, and helping your business win.
